title: Oscar-Nominated Director Chloé Zhao on Making Hamnet and Creative Process
text: Director Chloé Zhao discussed her creative process behind Hamnet, an adaptation of Maggie O'Farrell's novel about William Shakespeare and the love and grief inspiring Hamlet. The film earned eight Academy Award nominations, including Best Picture and Best Director, and won the Golden Globe for Best Motion Picture Drama. Zhao emphasized that identifying the film's core message proved essential, noting we cannot keep everything from the book, focusing instead on how two fundamentally different people understand each other through creativity. The adaptation required expanding Shakespeare's presence and incorporating more of the play itself. Zhao treats each filming day as a ceremony with proper opening and closing rituals. She explained that if you open your day properly, you are able to vibrate your entire company to the same energy, sometimes spending hours setting mood rather than shooting. The production incorporated dream interpretation sessions led by dream coach Kim Gillingham. Actors including Paul Mescal and Jessie Buckley brought dreams to set, using them to access that present state on the edge between knowing and not knowing.
